amendments.normalized_body: src/adapters/fs.rs:2927-2931 and src/adapters/fs.rs:3257-3261 explicitly disable snapshot-based `plan_hash` backfill whenever an existing lineage row is replayed or finalized, while the new auto-population at src/adapters/fs.rs:2956-2968 only runs for brand-new inserts. That leaves any pre-existing `TaskRunEntry { plan_hash: None }` permanently unlinked when callers keep omitting `plan_hash` on retry/completion, even if the milestone snapshot already has the canonical plan hash. A legacy row created by the old bug still completes with `plan_hash=None`, so Issue 3 is only fixed for new rows, not repairs.

src/adapters/fs.rs:2279-2339 still duplicates the journal detail schema instead of reusing the canonical payload types in src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:353-386, and the new regression tests at src/adapters/fs.rs:3768-3812 only round-trip one concrete payload. That does not guarantee drift detection: a future optional or conditionally serialized field can be added to the model payload without affecting these fixtures, so the duplicated fs structs still compile and the tests still pass. Amendment 1 asked for drift to fail at compile/test time; this guard is still example-based rather than structural.

final_accepted_amendments.normalized_body: src/adapters/fs.rs:2279-2339 still duplicates the journal detail schema instead of reusing the canonical payload types in src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:353-386, and the new regression tests at src/adapters/fs.rs:3768-3812 only round-trip one concrete payload. That does not guarantee drift detection: a future optional or conditionally serialized field can be added to the model payload without affecting these fixtures, so the duplicated fs structs still compile and the tests still pass. Amendment 1 asked for drift to fail at compile/test time; this guard is still example-based rather than structural.

payload.summary: Final review accepted 1 amendment(s); restart required.
